title: Android Bluetooth SPP to Arduino Serial Bridge
content: An HC-05 Bluetooth module paired with an Android app provides a wireless serial port profile (SPP) link to Arduino. Android sends ASCII command strings; Arduino parses bytes via SoftwareSerial or HardwareSerial.
extended_content: HC-05 parameters: 2.4 GHz BT 2.0 SPP, default baud 9600 (AT+UART=115200,0,0 to change), default PIN "1234" or "0000", pairing range 10 m. Android app options: 1) MIT App Inventor (free, no-code) with Bluetooth client block. 2) Arduino Bluetooth Controller (play store). 3) Custom Android app via BluetoothSocket (UUID 00001101-0000-1000-8000-00805F9B34FB for SPP). Protocol design: fixed-length packet e.g. STX + CMD(1 byte) + DATA(4 bytes) + ETX or ASCII "F050B-30\n" (forward 50%, turn -30%). Arduino loop: read Serial, parse command, set motor PWM. Latency: 10–30 ms. Range extension: HC-05 class 2, add external antenna for 50 m.
source: HC-05 AT Command Manual; MIT App Inventor BT Documentation; Android Developer Bluetooth Guide
